Re:
Farn PoLrrrcAL PnacrrcEs CorraMrssroN
428 J Street. Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
Iuly 17,2003
Virginia J. Bloom
Election Services Coordinator
Office of the County Clerk and Recorder
800 South Victoria Avenue
Ventura, CA 93009-1200
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. I-03-135
Dear Ms. Bloom:
You have requested advice concerning the duties of the Ventura County Elections
Division (the "County") under the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act').' Since your request seeks general guidance, we are treating it as a request for
informal assistance.
QUESTIONS
1. Does the County have the authority to request amendments to campaign
disclosure reports filed by a committee for elective state office controlled by a County of
Ventura officeholder?
2. How far back may a reviewing agency request amendments to campaign
disclosure statements?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Yes, as the filing officer for originally filed campaign statements, the County
is required to review the statements and request amendments for missing information.
2. The Act does not speciff a time limit on amending reports.
' Gorernmert Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18-997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)
ge


Our File No. I-03-135
Page2
F'ACTS
A county officeholder ran for state elective office in2000. As required under the
Act, the officeholder's controlled committee for the state office filed original campaign
disclosure reports with the county as well as with the Secretary of state.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires candidates to file periodic reports disclosing contributions
received and expenditures made for the purpose of seeking elective office. (Section
84100, et seq.) Under section 84215, elected state officeholders and candidates for state
elective office file the original3 and one copy of their campaign disclosure reports with
the Secretary of State, and file copies with specified county filing officers. County
officeholders and candidates for county office file the original and one copy of their
campaign disclosure reports with the county filing officer. (Section 84215(d).)
All committees controlled by an officeholder or candidate must file original
campaign disclosure reports in each location the officeholder or candidate is required to
file. (Barrett Advice Letter, Nos. 4-88-150 and 4-88-168.) In the circumstances
described in your letter, all of the county officeholder/state candidate's controlled
committees must file original campaign disclosure statements with the County of Ventura
as well as with the Secretary of State.
Filine Officer Duties
Section 81010 specifies the duties of filing officers:
"With respect to reports and statements filed with
him pursuant to this title, the filing officer shall:
(a) Supply the necessary forms and manuals
prescribed by the Commission;
@) Determine whether required documents have
beenfiled and, if so, whether they conform on theirfoce
with the requtrements of this tttle;
(c) Notiff promptly all persons and known
committees who have failed to file a report or statement in
the form and at the time required by this title;
(d) Report apparent violations of this title to the
appropriate agencies; and
(e) Compile and maintain a current list of all
reports and statements filed with this office.,, (Emphasis
added.)
Regulation I 8l 1 0 (copy enclosed) further describes the duties of filing officers
with regard to campaign disclosure statements and sets out specific information the filing
3 Under section 81004, original reports and statements filed under the Act must be signed under
penalty of perjury and verified by the filer.


Our File No. I-03-135
Page 3
officer must look for on original statements filed with him or her.a As the filing officer
for original campaign disclosure statements filed by Ventura County officeholders, you
are required to perform a detailed review and request needed amendments for all
statements filed by the officeholders and any of their controlled committees, including a
controlled committee for state office.
Please note, however, that subdivision (cX3) ofregulation 18110 does allow the
filing officer some discretion in requesting amendments:
"In those cases where the filing officer discovers in his or
her review of campaign statements that a candidate or
committee has filed an incorrect, incomplete or illegible
campaign statement ... he or she shall promptly notify the
candidate or committee of the effor or omission. However,
no notification is required in those cases in which the errors
or omissions are minor ones which do not recur throughout
the campaign statement. An eror or omission is minor if it
does not result in omission of the amount of an individual
contribution or expenditure ...."
Amendments
Nothing in the Act or Commission regulations specifically limits the time in
which a filing officer can request amendments. Section 81004.5 states that reports and
statements filed under the Act may be amended at any time and that amending an
incorrect or incomplete filing may be considered as evidence of good faith. T-ermination
of a committee does not exempt it from review or the requirement to file amendments if
information is missing or incomplete. Generally, a filing officer may request amendments
through the applicable retention period as specified in section g1009.
I trust this letter adequately addresses your concerns. Please contact me at 866-
275-3772 if you have additional questions.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
C^,t* tx)on,tlo,,t
By: CarlaWardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
LM:CW
Enclosures
a Where original statements are filed elsewhere, the regulation does not require the filing officer to
rwiew copies of campaign statements filed with him or her, but also does not prohi|it the filing offi."r
from performing a detailed review and requesting amendments. (Regulation t811g(bXlXC).)

disclosure statements?

formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)

QUESTION: 1. Does the County have the authority to request amendments to campaign
disclosure reports filed by a committee for elective state office controlled by a County of
Ventura officeholder?
2. How far back may a reviewing agency request amendments to campaign
disclosure statements?

CONCLUSION: 1. Yes, as the filing officer for originally filed campaign statements, the County
is required to review the statements and request amendments for missing information.
2. The Act does not speciff a time limit on amending reports.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)
